steps.title: Identify the key mechanism
steps.content: The formula's weighting—1.8 points per unit of land versus 1 per resident—is the decisive clue to who actually benefits from malapportionment.

steps.title: Apply the mechanism to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D names the formula's weighting and directly shows its result: less populated rural districts with large land tracts are overrepresented.
A contradicts the notes by saying rural districts are underrepresented instead, B wrongly claims more populated (urban) districts are overrepresented, and C just restates what malapportionment is. That matches choice D—D is correct.
